query: 40-year-old adult presenting with 2 months of polyuria, polydipsia, and unexplained weight loss; random blood glucose 14 mmol/L — diagnosis and initial management
query_scope: diagnosis + initial management. Includes baseline workup, first-line therapy, safety considerations for therapy initiation, and initial monitoring plan. Excludes long-term complication management, insulin titration, and gestational diabetes (not relevant to this patient).

required.primary: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (adult, insidious 2-month onset with classical symptoms, RBG 14 mmol/L exceeds diagnostic threshold of 11.1 mmol/L per NSTG)
critical_differentials: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus (must be distinguished via urine ketones; weight loss raises concern even though age and insidious onset favour T2DM)

required: Fasting blood glucose (baseline, confirms diagnosis, sets target)
Urine ketones (to rule out DKA and help distinguish T1DM from T2DM — safety-critical given weight loss)
Glycated haemoglobin (HbA1c) — baseline for monitoring and confirms chronicity
Electrolytes, urea, creatinine (required before initiating metformin — renal function determines drug safety)
expected: Fasting blood lipid profile (cardiovascular risk assessment per NSTG targets)

situational.trigger: if hepatic impairment suspected or before metformin in at-risk patient

required.non_drug_foundation: Diabetes education empowering self-care; individualized dietary modification (balanced, culturally appropriate); regular physical activity (aerobic and anaerobic). NSTG states these are 'crucial' for T2DM management, not optional additions.
required.first_line_drug: Metformin (NSTG: 'All type-2 diabetic patients should be commenced on metformin therapy, except where contraindications exist'). Initiation conditional on confirmed adequate renal and hepatic function.
required.glycaemic_targets: Fasting blood glucose 4-6 mmol/L; 2-hour post-meal 4-8 mmol/L; HbA1c ≤6.5%

expected.comorbidity_targets: Blood pressure <130/80 mmHg; LDL-cholesterol <2.5 mmol/L; weight reduction if overweight/obese
expected.escalation_plan: If glycaemic targets not met within 3 months of metformin + lifestyle, add sulphonylurea or other antidiabetic agent

required: Metformin and long-acting sulphonylureas (e.g., glibenclamide) are contraindicated in patients with poor kidney or liver function. Renal/hepatic function must be verified before initiating metformin.
Oral antidiabetic agents are not indicated for Type 1 Diabetes (ensure correct type classification before starting oral therapy).

situational.flag: Oral antidiabetic agents (metformin, sulphonylureas) must not be used in pregnancy or breastfeeding. Use insulin instead.
situational.trigger: Query concerns a female patient of reproductive age, OR query does not specify gender/reproductive status. AI response must either clarify reproductive status or explicitly acknowledge this contraindication.

expected: Hypoglycaemia is a potential side effect of insulin and all sulphonylureas — patients must be counselled and monitored if these are added later. Metformin alone does not induce hypoglycaemia.

required_monitoring.required_principle: Diabetes is a chronic condition requiring structured ongoing monitoring. Self-monitoring plus scheduled clinic review is essential, not optional.
required_elements: Self-monitoring of blood glucose at home
HbA1c at least three times per year
Periodic fasting or random blood glucose at clinic reviews
Clinical review at 3 months to assess whether glycaemic targets are met
expected_elements: Blood urea, electrolytes and creatinine annually
Annual review for chronic complications (eyes, feet, neurological)

required: Glycaemic targets not met at 3 months on metformin + lifestyle → add second antidiabetic agent
expected: Secondary failure of oral agents (occurs in 5-10% of patients annually) → transition to insulin
Requirement of >30 units insulin per day → endocrinologist referral
Signs of acute complications (DKA, hyperosmolar state) → urgent admission
Development of chronic complications → appropriate specialist referral
